steps.title: Find the semicircle radius
steps.content: Since $K$ is the midpoint of $\overline{JL}$ and $JK=13$, then $KL=13$ and $JL=26$. Point $L$ is the center of the semicircle, so $LM$ is a radius and $JM$ is a diameter. Because $JM=JL+LM$, $$2r=26+r,$$ so $r=26$.

steps.title: Find the rectangle area
steps.content: The rectangle's base is $KM=KL+LM=13+26=39$, and its height is $14$. Its area is $$39\cdot14=546.$$

steps.title: Add the semicircle area
steps.content: The semicircle area is $$\frac{1}{2}\pi(26)^2=338\pi.$$ Therefore the total area is $$546+338\pi.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: $546+338\pi$ matches Choice C — $546 + 338\pi$. The choices with $676\pi$ use the full circle area instead of the semicircle area.
